2606.22470.title: PRIME: Evaluating Prompt Resolution Under Incompatible Instructions in LLMs
2606.22470.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) often encounter conflicting prompts, although current instruction following benchmarks assess those meta-instructions in isolation, limiting the insights about how models process conflicting instructions. We introduce a framework \textit{PRIME}(\textit{Prompt Resolution under Incompatible Meta-Instructions Evaluation}) to analyze behavior of LLMs when provided with conflicting instructions. \textit{PRIME} purposefully produces calibrated conflicts across response length, output format, and reasoning; classifying model responses with a deterministic behavioral taxonomy. We are evaluating five instruction tuned open weight LLMs in two distinct settings, balanced and naturally distributed. The conclusion we reach upon analysis is that conflict type is more significant in affecting behavior than model scale, and various failure modes across different categories of conflict. Our findings emphasize the value of developing conflict awareness and suggest ability of LLM to follow instructions cannot be assessed through isolated constraints alone.
2606.22470.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）經常遇到相互矛盾的提示，儘管目前的指令遵循基準在孤立的情況下評估這些元指令，限制了對模型如何處理矛盾指令的洞察。我們引入了一個框架 \textit{PRIME}（\textit{不相容元指令評估下的提示解決}）來分析 LLM 在提供矛盾指令時的行為。\textit{PRIME} 有意地在回應長度、輸出格式和推理上產生校準的衝突；使用確定性的行為分類法對模型回應進行分類。我們正在兩個不同的設置中評估五個經過指令調整的開放權重 LLM，這些設置分別是平衡和自然分佈。我們在分析後得出的結論是，衝突類型在影響行為方面比模型規模更為重要，並且在不同衝突類別中存在各種失敗模式。我們的研究結果強調了發展衝突意識的價值，並建議 LLM 跟隨指令的能力不能僅通過孤立的約束來評估。
2606.22470.author: Tehreem Javed et.al.
2606.22470.authors: Tehreem Javed, Shumaim Fatimah, Masooma Bakhtiari, Gibrail Islam, Mehwish Fatima
